message:
refactor(cli): replace two-step approve-then-retry flow with inline --approval-code on send

The previous approval workflow required users to run "ft approve <code>"
as a separate step to consume the token, then retry the original command.
This was error-prone and created a TOCTOU race where the token could be
consumed between validation and the retry.

The new flow issues the approval token on the first gated send and
returns it with instructions to retry the same command with
--approval-code <code>. The token is consumed atomically at send time,
eliminating the race condition.

Changes:
- Add --approval-code flag to both ft send and ft robot send commands
- Implement resolve_inline_send_approval() that:
  - Passes through non-approval decisions unchanged
  - Consumes provided approval codes via ApprovalStore::consume_with_context
  - Issues new tokens when no code is provided, attaching retry instructions
- Refactor evaluate_approve() to validate-only (no longer consumes tokens):
  - Remove the consume_approval_token_by_code call and audit record
  - Return token metadata for display regardless of --dry-run flag
  - Mark actor_kind parameter as unused since audit recording is removed
- Update ft approve help text: "Submit" → "Validate", add see-also
  references to ft send --approval-code and ft commit
- Update robot quick-start: recovery instructions now say "retry with
  --approval-code" instead of "use ft approve"
- Add test approve_validation_does_not_consume_token verifying the
  new validate-only behavior
- Update existing tests to expect consumed_at=None from evaluate_approve

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
